name: reorder
$comment: Canonical reorder demo (v5.0 rewrite — replaces the old select-then-place 'move-start/move-before/move-to-end' prototype). Items live in groups (backlog/active/archive; archive starts empty). Per-row identity is encoded in the action name (Phase 6 / WIRE-07); no bind paths (no input fields). Two blessed patterns, both from existing primitives: (1) Up/Down buttons reorder WITHIN a group — first row's Up + last row's Down are disabled:true; (2) a Move… button opens a ModalNode (dismissAction:'move-close', no same-named footer per the AGENTS.md gotcha) listing the OTHER groups, and move-to-<folderKey>-<id> RELOCATES the item (append to the target group). Drag-and-drop is rejected (mouse-only → not agent/keyboard-drivable). The steps exercise: normal Up/Down, the server-side CLAMP when an agent dispatches a disabled Up/Down directly (move-up on a first item / single-item group = no-op — disabled is a client-only hint, the server must be safe), move-open→move-to relocation, move-close cancel, and populating the initially-empty archive group. Every step's response (grouped view-shape + disabled flags + optional modal) is byte-diffed across dotnet-reorder / bun-reorder / node.
